input persona: A historian specialized in Byzantine and Seljuk architecture, focusing on the transformation and adaptive reuse of religious buildings across different empires and cultures.
synthesized text: As I stand before the majestic Hagia Sophia, I am struck by the intricate mosaics that adorn its walls, a testament to the Byzantine Empire's grandeur. How did the Seljuk Turks, in turn, influence the architectural style of the mosques built during their reign, and what can we learn from the adaptive reuse of these sacred spaces across empires and cultures?
